title: Filter manipulation: reader/generator/writer
context: PoECraft v1. Implements the loot-filter manipulation layer in poecraft/filter/{reader,generator,writer}.py. Highlight-only behavior (settled in grill): emit exactly one Show rule — strong style (background/text/border color, MinimapIcon, PlayEffect) — per MISSING item class; NEVER emit Hide; classes that aren't missing are left entirely to the user's existing filter. Consumes RecipeStatus.missing_classes from the recipe-core plan. Section is wrapped in marker comments, injected/replaced idempotently, and removable. Fully unit-testable against tmp_path .filter files — no real filter needed. Depends on recipe-core scaffolding (pytest, tests/, jj repo) and poecraft/recipe/types.py (ItemClass, RecipeType, RECIPE_ILVL_RANGES) which already exists.
architecture: poecraft/filter/reader.py: MARKER_START, MARKER_END; read_filter(path)->str; split_filter(content)->tuple[before, section, after] (no markers => before='', section='', after=whole). poecraft/filter/generator.py: CLASS_COLORS: dict[ItemClass, {bg,text,border}] (RGBA hex from CRE defaults); CLASS_FILTER_NAMES: dict[ItemClass, str] with quoted display names ('Body Armours', 'One Hand Weapons', 'Two Hand Weapons', plus singulars); DEFAULT_STYLE = ['HasInfluence None', 'Sockets < 6', 'LinkedSockets < 5']; _parse_hex('#RRGGBBAA')->tuple[int,int,int,int]; generate_rule(item_class, recipe_type, include_identified=False)->str (Show, Class '<name>', Rarity Rare, [Identified False unless include_identified], ItemLevel >=/<= from RECIPE_ILVL_RANGES, DEFAULT_STYLE, Set*Color, SetFontSize 40, MinimapIcon 0 Yellow Circle, PlayEffect Yellow Temp); generate_section(missing_classes, recipe_type, include_identified=False)->str (markers + one Show rule per missing class, sorted; never Hide). poecraft/filter/writer.py: write_filter(path, content); update_filter(path, missing_classes, recipe_type, include_identified=False)->bool; remove_chaos_section(path)->bool.

red.description: In tests/test_filter_reader.py, split_filter on content with markers returns (before, section, after); content with no markers returns ('', '', whole). Fails (module missing).

green.description: Implement poecraft/filter/reader.py: MARKER_START, MARKER_END, read_filter(path)->str, split_filter(content)->tuple[str,str,str]. Pass.

steps.name: generate_rule — single Show rule for Rings/chaos

red.description: generate_rule(ItemClass.RINGS, RecipeType.CHAOS) returns a string containing: Show, Class "Rings", Rarity Rare, Identified False, ItemLevel >= 60, ItemLevel <= 74, HasInfluence None, Sockets < 6, LinkedSockets < 5, SetBackgroundColor 255 150 0 255, SetFontSize 40, MinimapIcon 0 Yellow Circle, PlayEffect Yellow Temp. Fails.

green.description: Implement CLASS_COLORS, CLASS_FILTER_NAMES, DEFAULT_STYLE, generate_rule. Pass.

steps.name: generate_rule — include_identified omits Identified line; recipe ilvl ranges

red.description: generate_rule(RINGS, REGAL, include_identified=True) contains ItemLevel >= 75 and NO 'Identified' line; generate_rule(RINGS, CHANCE) contains ItemLevel <= 59. Fails.

green.description: Wire RECIPE_ILVL_RANGES + conditional Identified line. Pass.

red.description: generate_rule(BODY_ARMOURS,...) contains Class "Body Armours"; ONE_HAND_WEAPONS -> Class "One Hand Weapons"; TWO_HAND_WEAPONS -> Class "Two Hand Weapons". Fails.

green.description: Complete CLASS_FILTER_NAMES for all 9 classes. Pass.

steps.name: generate_section — highlight-only (missing classes only, never Hide, markers)

red.description: generate_section({RINGS, BOOTS}, CHAOS) contains exactly one Show rule for Rings and one for Boots, wrapped in MARKER_START..MARKER_END, and contains NO 'Hide' and NO rule for any non-missing class. Fails.

refactor.description: sort missing classes for deterministic output.

red.description: Write a dummy .filter (no markers) to tmp_path; update_filter(path, {RINGS}, CHAOS, False) -> True; file now contains markers + Rings Show rule + original content. Fails.

green.description: Implement poecraft/filter/writer.py: write_filter, update_filter (read -> split -> generate_section -> splice -> write). Pass.

red.description: Call update_filter twice on the same file -> the Rings Show rule appears exactly once; before/after content intact. Fails.
After update_filter then remove_chaos_section -> file has no markers, no section, original content intact. Fails.

steps.name: integration — missing_classes from recipe-core drives the filter

red.description: In tests/test_filter_integration.py, build EnhancedItems via recipe-core, compute missing_classes, call update_filter on a tmp file, assert the written filter Shows the missing classes and Hides none. Fails.

design.currentState: poecraft/filter has only an empty __init__.py. reader.py, generator.py, writer.py do not exist. recipe-core (types, classifier) is complete; pytest + tests/ + jj repo exist from recipe-core.
design.desiredState: filter/reader.py, generator.py, writer.py implemented and unit-tested. update_filter injects/replaces a highlight-only section; remove_chaos_section cleans it. Full pytest suite green.
design.patterns: TDD red-green-refactor. Pure functions; tmp_path .filter files as fixtures. Hex colors parsed to RGBA ints. Stable (sorted) rule order in generate_section for deterministic output.
design.decisions: Highlight-only: Show + strong style for missing classes; never Hide; non-missing classes left to the user filter. Quoted multi-word Class names. No always_active_classes param. Section wrapped in MARKER_START/MARKER_END.
